arguments.file_text:
# Flink Checkpoint Coordination Architecture

## Files Examined

- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/checkpoint/CheckpointCoordinator.java` — Central JobManager-side coordinator: triggers checkpoints, tracks pending checkpoints, receives acknowledgments, drives completion
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/checkpoint/PendingCheckpoint.java` — Mutable in-flight checkpoint state: tracks which tasks/coordinators/master-hooks have not yet acknowledged; finalizes into CompletedCheckpoint
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/checkpoint/CompletedCheckpoint.java` — Immutable record of a successfully completed checkpoint with all operator state handles and external metadata pointer
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/checkpoint/CheckpointCoordinatorGateway.java` — RPC interface through which TaskManagers send `acknowledgeCheckpoint` and `declineCheckpoint` messages back to the JobMaster
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/checkpoint/CheckpointPlan.java` — Describes which tasks to trigger (sources), which to wait for (all), and which to commit to after completion
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/checkpoint/DefaultCheckpointPlanCalculator.java` — Computes the CheckpointPlan from the ExecutionGraph topology
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/checkpoint/OperatorCoordinatorCheckpoints.java` — Triggers and acknowledges OperatorCoordinator-side checkpoints before task barriers are sent
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/checkpoint/CheckpointRequestDecider.java` — Decides whether a new checkpoint trigger request should execute immediately or be queued, enforcing max-concurrent and min-pause constraints
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/checkpoint/CheckpointFailureManager.java` — Tracks checkpoint failures and decides when to fail the job
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/executiongraph/Execution.java` — Represents a running task attempt; dispatches `triggerCheckpoint` RPC to the TaskManager via `TaskManagerGateway`
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/jobmaster/RpcTaskManagerGateway.java` — Implements `TaskManagerGateway` by delegating to `TaskExecutorGateway` (Akka/Pekko RPC)
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/jobmanager/slots/TaskManagerGateway.java` — Interface for JobManager→TaskManager RPC calls including `triggerCheckpoint`
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/taskexecutor/TaskExecutorGateway.java` — Interface for the TaskExecutor RPC endpoint; receives `triggerCheckpoint` from the JobMaster
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/tasks/StreamTask.java` — Base class for all streaming tasks; implements `CheckpointableTask`; routes `triggerCheckpointOnBarrier` into `SubtaskCheckpointCoordinatorImpl.checkpointState()`
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/tasks/SubtaskCheckpointCoordinatorImpl.java` — Task-side checkpoint orchestrator: broadcasts the barrier downstream, takes synchronous state snapshots, submits async finalization
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/tasks/AsyncCheckpointRunnable.java` — Async runnable that finalizes operator snapshot futures and calls `reportCompletedSnapshotStates` to send the ACK
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/state/TaskStateManagerImpl.java` — Bridges the task side to the JobMaster: stores local state and calls `checkpointResponder.acknowledgeCheckpoint()` (RPC)
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/io/checkpointing/CheckpointBarrierHandler.java` — Abstract base for barrier handlers; calls `toNotifyOnCheckpoint.triggerCheckpointOnBarrier()` when a checkpoint is ready to trigger
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/io/checkpointing/SingleCheckpointBarrierHandler.java` — EXACTLY_ONCE barrier handler; blocks aligned channels, uses a `BarrierHandlerState` state machine; supports aligned, unaligned, and alternating modes
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/io/checkpointing/CheckpointBarrierTracker.java` — AT_LEAST_ONCE barrier handler; tracks barrier counts without blocking channels; supports up to 50 concurrent in-flight checkpoints
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/io/checkpointing/CheckpointedInputGate.java` — Wraps the network input gate; intercepts `CheckpointBarrier`, `CancelCheckpointMarker`, and `EndOfPartitionEvent` and routes them to the `CheckpointBarrierHandler`
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/io/checkpointing/InputProcessorUtil.java` — Factory that selects the correct `CheckpointBarrierHandler` implementation based on checkpointing mode and alignment configuration
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/io/checkpointing/BarrierHandlerState.java` — State machine interface used by `SingleCheckpointBarrierHandler`; transitions on `barrierReceived`, `announcementReceived`, `alignedCheckpointTimeout`, `abort`
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/io/checkpointing/WaitingForFirstBarrier.java` — Initial state for aligned `SingleCheckpointBarrierHandler`
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/io/checkpointing/CollectingBarriers.java` — Aligned collecting state; blocks channels that have sent barriers
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/io/checkpointing/AlternatingWaitingForFirstBarrier.java` — Initial state for alternating (aligned-with-timeout) mode
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/io/checkpointing/AlternatingCollectingBarriers.java` — Aligned phase of alternating mode; can time out to unaligned
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/io/checkpointing/AlternatingCollectingBarriersUnaligned.java` — Unaligned phase of alternating mode
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/checkpoint/CheckpointCoordinatorDeActivator.java` — `JobStatusListener` that starts/stops periodic checkpoint scheduling when the job transitions to/from RUNNING
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/checkpoint/hooks/MasterHooks.java` — Utility for triggering `MasterTriggerRestoreHook` instances during checkpoint initiation

---

## Dependency Chain

1. **Entry point (periodic)**: `CheckpointCoordinator.ScheduledTrigger.run()` → `triggerCheckpoint(isPeriodic=true)`
   - File: `CheckpointCoordinator.java`

2. **Entry point (external)**: `CheckpointCoordinator.triggerCheckpoint(boolean)` or `triggerSavepoint()` → `triggerCheckpointFromCheckpointThread()`
   - File: `CheckpointCoordinator.java`

3. **Request gating**: `triggerCheckpointFromCheckpointThread()` submits to timer thread → `triggerCheckpoint(props, location, isPeriodic)` → `chooseRequestToExecute(request)` via `CheckpointRequestDecider`
   - File: `CheckpointCoordinator.java`, `CheckpointRequestDecider.java`

4. **Trigger pipeline**: `startTriggeringCheckpoint(request)`:
   a. `checkpointPlanCalculator.calculateCheckpointPlan()` → `CheckpointPlan`
   b. `checkpointIdCounter.getAndIncrement()` → unique checkpoint ID
   c. `createPendingCheckpoint(...)` → `PendingCheckpoint` registered in `pendingCheckpoints` map; `CheckpointCanceller` scheduled for timeout
   d. `initializeCheckpointLocation(...)` → `CheckpointStorageLocation`
   e. `OperatorCoordinatorCheckpoints.triggerAndAcknowledgeAllCoordinatorCheckpointsWithCompletion(...)` → coordinator states captured
   f. `snapshotMasterState(checkpoint)` → `MasterHooks.triggerHook(...)` for each master hook
   g. `triggerCheckpointRequest(request, timestamp, checkpoint)` → `triggerTasks(...)`
   - File: `CheckpointCoordinator.java`, `PendingCheckpoint.java`, `OperatorCoordinatorCheckpoints.java`

5. **RPC dispatch to source tasks**: `triggerTasks()` iterates `checkpoint.getCheckpointPlan().getTasksToTrigger()` → `execution.triggerCheckpoint(checkpointId, timestamp, checkpointOptions)`
   - File: `CheckpointCoordinator.java`, `Execution.java`

6. **TaskManager gateway**: `Execution.triggerCheckpoint()` → `slot.getTaskManagerGateway().triggerCheckpoint(attemptId, jobId, checkpointId, timestamp, options)` → `RpcTaskManagerGateway` → `TaskExecutorGateway.triggerCheckpoint()` (RPC)
   - File: `Execution.java`, `RpcTaskManagerGateway.java`, `TaskExecutorGateway.java`

7. **Task receives trigger**: `TaskExecutor` finds the `Task` by `ExecutionAttemptID` → `task.triggerCheckpointBarrier(checkpointId, timestamp, options)` → `StreamTask.triggerCheckpointAsync()` → `performCheckpoint()` → `subtaskCheckpointCoordinator.checkpointState(...)`
   - File: `StreamTask.java`, `SubtaskCheckpointCoordinatorImpl.java`

8. **Barrier broadcast (source task)**: `SubtaskCheckpointCoordinatorImpl.checkpointState()`:
   - Step 1: `operatorChain.prepareSnapshotPreBarrier(checkpointId)`
   - Step 2: Creates `CheckpointBarrier` → `operatorChain.broadcastEvent(checkpointBarrier)` — injects barrier into all output channels
   - Step 3: For unaligned/alternating: `channelStateWriter.finishOutput(checkpointId)` — captures in-flight output buffers
   - Step 4: `takeSnapshotSync(...)` → `operatorChain.snapshotState(...)` — synchronous operator state snapshot
   - Step 5: `finishAndReportAsync(...)` → submits `AsyncCheckpointRunnable` to async thread pool
   - File: `SubtaskCheckpointCoordinatorImpl.java`

9. **Barrier propagation through network**: Barriers flow through Flink's network stack (result partitions → input channels) to downstream tasks.

10. **Downstream barrier reception**: `CheckpointedInputGate.pollNext()` intercepts `CheckpointBarrier` events → `barrierHandler.processBarrier(barrier, channelInfo, isRpcTriggered)`
    - File: `CheckpointedInputGate.java`

11. **Barrier alignment (EXACTLY_ONCE)**: `SingleCheckpointBarrierHandler.processBarrier()`:
    - `checkNewCheckpoint(barrier)` — initializes new checkpoint tracking, optionally registers alignment timer
    - `markCheckpointAlignedAndTransformState(channelInfo, barrier, state -> state.barrierReceived(...))` — state machine transition
    - Blocks the channel that sent the barrier (via `CheckpointableInput`)
    - When `alignedChannels.size() == targetChannelCount` → `allBarriersReceivedFuture.complete(null)` and triggers checkpoint
    - File: `SingleCheckpointBarrierHandler.java`, `BarrierHandlerState.java`

12. **Barrier tracking (AT_LEAST_ONCE)**: `CheckpointBarrierTracker.processBarrier()`:
    - Maintains `ArrayDeque<CheckpointBarrierCount>` for up to 50 concurrent checkpoints
    - Does NOT block channels
    - When all channels counted → `triggerCheckpointOnAligned()` → `notifyCheckpoint(barrier)`
    - File: `CheckpointBarrierTracker.java`

13. **Downstream task checkpoint trigger**: `CheckpointBarrierHandler.notifyCheckpoint(barrier)` → `toNotifyOnCheckpoint.triggerCheckpointOnBarrier(checkpointMetaData, options, metrics)` → `StreamTask.triggerCheckpointOnBarrier()` → `performCheckpoint()` → `subtaskCheckpointCoordinator.checkpointState(...)` (same flow as step 8, but without barrier broadcast since this is a downstream task)
    - File: `CheckpointBarrierHandler.java`, `StreamTask.java`

14. **Async snapshot finalization**: `AsyncCheckpointRunnable.run()`:
    - `finalizeNonFinishedSnapshots()` — waits for all `OperatorSnapshotFutures` to complete, collects `TaskStateSnapshot`
    - `reportCompletedSnapshotStates(jmSnapshot, localSnapshot, asyncDuration)` → `taskEnvironment.getTaskStateManager().reportTaskStateSnapshots(...)`
    - File: `AsyncCheckpointRunnable.java`

15. **ACK sent to JobMaster**: `TaskStateManagerImpl.reportTaskStateSnapshots()`:
    - `localStateStore.storeLocalState(checkpointId, localState)` — stores local state copy
    - `checkpointResponder.acknowledgeCheckpoint(jobId, executionAttemptID, checkpointId, metrics, acknowledgedState)` — RPC call via `CheckpointCoordinatorGateway`
    - File: `TaskStateManagerImpl.java`, `CheckpointCoordinatorGateway.java`

16. **ACK received at coordinator**: `CheckpointCoordinator.receiveAcknowledgeMessage(AcknowledgeCheckpoint)`:
    - Looks up `PendingCheckpoint` by checkpoint ID
    - `checkpoint.acknowledgeTask(executionAttemptId, subtaskState, metrics)` → removes task from `notYetAcknowledgedTasks`, stores `OperatorSubtaskState`
    - If `checkpoint.isFullyAcknowledged()` → `completePendingCheckpoint(checkpoint)`
    - File: `CheckpointCoordinator.java`, `PendingCheckpoint.java`

17. **Checkpoint completion**: `completePendingCheckpoint(pendingCheckpoint)`:
    - `finalizeCheckpoint(pendingCheckpoint)` → `pendingCheckpoint.finalizeCheckpoint(...)`:
      - Writes `CheckpointMetadata` via `Checkpoints.storeCheckpointMetadata(savepoint, out)`
      - Creates `CompletedCheckpoint` with all operator states and finalized storage location
    - `addCompletedCheckpointToStoreAndSubsumeOldest(...)` → `completedCheckpointStore.addCheckpointAndSubsumeOldestOne(...)` — persists to HA store, subsumes older checkpoints
    - `reportCompletedCheckpoint(completedCheckpoint)` — updates stats tracker
    - `pendingCheckpoint.getCompletionFuture().complete(completedCheckpoint)` — fulfills the original trigger future
    - `cleanupAfterCompletedCheckpoint(...)` → `sendAcknowledgeMessages(...)` — notifies all tasks via `execution.notifyCheckpointOnComplete()` (RPC) and all OperatorCoordinators
    - File: `CheckpointCoordinator.java`, `PendingCheckpoint.java`, `CompletedCheckpoint.java`

---

## Analysis

### Design Patterns

**1. Distributed Two-Phase Commit (2PC)**

Flink's checkpoint protocol is a distributed 2PC:
- **Phase 1 (Prepare/Vote)**: The coordinator triggers barriers; each task takes a state snapshot and sends an `AcknowledgeCheckpoint` message (its "vote to commit"). The `PendingCheckpoint` accumulates these votes.
- **Phase 2 (Commit)**: Once all tasks have acknowledged (`isFullyAcknowledged()`), the coordinator finalizes the checkpoint metadata, persists it to the `CompletedCheckpointStore`, and broadcasts `notifyCheckpointComplete` to all tasks. Tasks then call `notifyCheckpointComplete` on their operators (e.g., Kafka consumer commits offsets).

**2. State Machine for Barrier Alignment**

`SingleCheckpointBarrierHandler` uses the State pattern (`BarrierHandlerState` interface) to manage the complex lifecycle of barrier alignment:
- `WaitingForFirstBarrier` → `CollectingBarriers` (aligned mode)
- `AlternatingWaitingForFirstBarrier` → `AlternatingCollectingBarriers` → `AlternatingCollectingBarriersUnaligned` (alternating mode, can time out from aligned to unaligned)
- `AlternatingWaitingForFirstBarrierUnaligned` → `AlternatingCollectingBarriersUnaligned` (strict unaligned mode)

State transitions are triggered by `barrierReceived()`, `announcementReceived()`, `alignedCheckpointTimeout()`, and `abort()`.

**3. Observer/Listener Pattern**

`CheckpointBarrierHandler` holds a reference to `CheckpointableTask` (implemented by `StreamTask`). When all barriers are received, it calls `toNotifyOnCheckpoint.triggerCheckpointOnBarrier()`, decoupling barrier tracking from checkpoint execution.

**4. Gateway/RPC Abstraction**

Three gateway layers separate concerns:
- `TaskManagerGateway` (JobManager→TaskManager): `triggerCheckpoint`, `abortCheckpoint`
- `TaskExecutorGateway` (same, RPC-level): implemented by Pekko/Akka actors
- `CheckpointCoordinatorGateway` (TaskManager→JobManager): `acknowledgeCheckpoint`, `declineCheckpoint`

**5. Future-Based Async Pipeline**

`CheckpointCoordinator.startTriggeringCheckpoint()` chains `CompletableFuture` stages: plan calculation → ID allocation → pending checkpoint creation → storage location init → coordinator checkpoints → master hooks → task trigger. This avoids blocking the coordinator thread on I/O.

**6. Template Method**

`CheckpointBarrierHandler` defines the algorithm skeleton (`notifyCheckpoint`, `notifyAbort`, alignment tracking) while subclasses (`SingleCheckpointBarrierHandler`, `CheckpointBarrierTracker`) implement the barrier processing logic.

### Component Responsibilities

| Component | Responsibility |
|-----------|---------------|
| `CheckpointCoordinator` | Orchestrates the entire checkpoint lifecycle: trigger, track, complete, abort |
| `PendingCheckpoint` | Mutable in-flight state; accumulates task ACKs; finalizes to `CompletedCheckpoint` |
| `CompletedCheckpoint` | Immutable record of a successful checkpoint; persisted to HA store |
| `CheckpointPlanCalculator` | Determines which tasks to trigger vs. wait for based on topology |
| `Execution` | Dispatches trigger RPC to the TaskManager for a specific task attempt |
| `SubtaskCheckpointCoordinatorImpl` | Task-side: broadcasts barrier, takes snapshot, submits async finalization |
| `AsyncCheckpointRunnable` | Finalizes async snapshot futures; sends ACK via `TaskStateManagerImpl` |
| `TaskStateManagerImpl` | Bridges task to coordinator: stores local state, sends `acknowledgeCheckpoint` RPC |
| `CheckpointedInputGate` | Intercepts barrier events from the network layer |
| `SingleCheckpointBarrierHandler` | EXACTLY_ONCE barrier alignment with channel blocking and state machine |
| `CheckpointBarrierTracker` | AT_LEAST_ONCE barrier counting without channel blocking |
| `InputProcessorUtil` | Factory selecting the correct barrier handler based on configuration |

### Data Flow Description

```
JobManager (CheckpointCoordinator)
  │
  ├─ 1. Calculate plan (which tasks to trigger/wait for)
  ├─ 2. Allocate checkpoint ID
  ├─ 3. Create PendingCheckpoint (register in map, schedule timeout)
  ├─ 4. Initialize storage location
  ├─ 5. Trigger OperatorCoordinator checkpoints (async)
  ├─ 6. Trigger master hook snapshots (async)
  └─ 7. Send triggerCheckpoint RPC to source tasks
         │
         ▼
TaskManager (StreamTask / SubtaskCheckpointCoordinatorImpl)
  │
  ├─ 8. prepareSnapshotPreBarrier (operators)
  ├─ 9. Broadcast CheckpointBarrier to output channels ──────────────────────┐
  ├─ 10. [Unaligned] Capture in-flight output buffers                        │
  ├─ 11. takeSnapshotSync (operator state)                                   │
  └─ 12. Submit AsyncCheckpointRunnable                                      │
           │                                                                  │
           ▼                                                                  ▼
    Async thread pool                                          Network (barriers flow downstream)
    ├─ Finalize OperatorSnapshotFutures                               │
    └─ reportCompletedSnapshotStates                                  ▼
         │                                              Downstream TaskManager
         ▼                                              (CheckpointedInputGate)
    TaskStateManagerImpl                                │
    └─ acknowledgeCheckpoint RPC ──────────────────────┤ CheckpointBarrierHandler
                                                        │ ├─ [EXACTLY_ONCE] SingleCheckpointBarrierHandler
                                                        │ │   Block channels, wait for all barriers
                                                        │ └─ [AT_LEAST_ONCE] CheckpointBarrierTracker
                                                        │     Count barriers, no blocking
                                                        │
                                                        └─ triggerCheckpointOnBarrier
                                                             └─ (same snapshot flow as source task)
                                                                  └─ acknowledgeCheckpoint RPC
                                                                       │
                                                                       ▼
JobManager (CheckpointCoordinator.receiveAcknowledgeMessage)
  │
  ├─ PendingCheckpoint.acknowledgeTask() [remove from notYetAcknowledgedTasks]
  └─ If isFullyAcknowledged():
       ├─ finalizeCheckpoint() → write metadata → CompletedCheckpoint
       ├─ addCheckpointAndSubsumeOldestOne() → persist to HA store
       ├─ complete onCompletionPromise
       └─ sendAcknowledgeMessages() → notifyCheckpointOnComplete RPC to all tasks
```

### Interface Contracts Between Components

- **`CheckpointCoordinator` ↔ `PendingCheckpoint`**: Coordinator creates and owns `PendingCheckpoint`; calls `acknowledgeTask()`, `isFullyAcknowledged()`, `finalizeCheckpoint()`. `PendingCheckpoint` is `@NotThreadSafe` and must be accessed under the coordinator's `lock`.
- **`CheckpointBarrierHandler` ↔ `CheckpointableTask`**: Handler calls `triggerCheckpointOnBarrier()` and `abortCheckpointOnBarrier()` on the task. The task must not block these calls.
- **`SubtaskCheckpointCoordinatorImpl` ↔ `OperatorChain`**: Coordinator calls `prepareSnapshotPreBarrier()`, `broadcastEvent()`, `snapshotState()` on the chain. The chain iterates operators in reverse order for snapshots.
- **`AsyncCheckpointRunnable` ↔ `TaskStateManagerImpl`**: Runnable calls `reportTaskStateSnapshots()` with the finalized `TaskStateSnapshot`. `TaskStateManagerImpl` is responsible for the RPC call.
- **`TaskStateManagerImpl` ↔ `CheckpointCoordinatorGateway`**: `acknowledgeCheckpoint()` is a fire-and-forget RPC; the coordinator processes it asynchronously.

### Aligned vs. Unaligned Checkpoint Handling

**Aligned (`SingleCheckpointBarrierHandler.aligned()`)**:
- Initial state: `WaitingForFirstBarrier`
- On first barrier: transitions to `CollectingBarriers`, blocks the channel that sent the barrier
- On subsequent barriers: blocks each channel, counts aligned channels
- When `alignedChannels.size() == targetChannelCount`: triggers checkpoint, unblocks all channels
- Guarantees exactly-once by ensuring no records from after the barrier are processed before the snapshot
- Drawback: can cause back-pressure if one channel is slow

**Unaligned (`SingleCheckpointBarrierHandler.unaligned()`)**:
- Initial state: `AlternatingWaitingForFirstBarrierUnaligned`
- On first barrier announcement (`processBarrierAnnouncement()`): immediately triggers checkpoint
- Captures in-flight buffers in all channels (input and output) as part of checkpoint state
- Does NOT block channels; processing continues while snapshot is taken
- Eliminates alignment back-pressure at the cost of larger checkpoint size

**Alternating (`SingleCheckpointBarrierHandler.alternating()`)**:
- Starts in aligned mode (`AlternatingWaitingForFirstBarrier`)
- If alignment completes within `alignedCheckpointTimeout`: stays aligned
- If timeout fires (`alignedCheckpointTimeout()` on state): transitions to `AlternatingCollectingBarriersUnaligned`, switches to unaligned mode mid-checkpoint
- Balances latency (aligned when fast) with throughput (unaligned when slow)

**`CheckpointBarrierTracker` (AT_LEAST_ONCE)**:
- Maintains a queue of `CheckpointBarrierCount` objects (up to 50)
- Does NOT block any channels; records continue flowing
- Ignores `processBarrierAnnouncement()` entirely
- When all channels counted for a checkpoint ID: triggers checkpoint
- Older checkpoints are subsumed when a newer one completes

---

## Summary

Flink's checkpoint coordination implements a distributed two-phase commit protocol driven by the `CheckpointCoordinator` on the JobManager. In Phase 1, the coordinator creates a `PendingCheckpoint`, triggers source tasks via RPC (`Execution` → `TaskManagerGateway` → `TaskExecutorGateway`), and each source task injects a `CheckpointBarrier` into its output channels via `SubtaskCheckpointCoordinatorImpl.checkpointState()`; downstream tasks receive barriers through `CheckpointedInputGate` and either align them (blocking channels until all barriers arrive, via `SingleCheckpointBarrierHandler`) or simply count them (via `CheckpointBarrierTracker`), then take their own state snapshots and send `AcknowledgeCheckpoint` RPCs back to the coordinator via `TaskStateManagerImpl`. In Phase 2, once `PendingCheckpoint.isFullyAcknowledged()` is true (all tasks, OperatorCoordinators, and master hooks have acknowledged), the coordinator calls `finalizeCheckpoint()` to write metadata and create a `CompletedCheckpoint`, persists it to the `CompletedCheckpointStore`, and broadcasts `notifyCheckpointComplete` to all tasks so they can commit external side effects (e.g., Kafka offsets). The aligned vs. unaligned distinction is encapsulated in the `BarrierHandlerState` state machine within `SingleCheckpointBarrierHandler`, where aligned mode blocks channels to prevent out-of-order processing, unaligned mode captures in-flight buffers and triggers immediately on the first barrier announcement, and alternating mode starts aligned but can time out to unaligned to avoid back-pressure.

## Summary of Findings

### Complete Checkpoint Coordination Flow

**Phase 1 — Trigger & Barrier Propagation:**
1. `CheckpointCoordinator.triggerCheckpoint()` creates a `PendingCheckpoint`, triggers OperatorCoordinator/master-hook snapshots, then sends `triggerCheckpoint` RPCs to source tasks via `Execution` → `TaskManagerGateway` → `TaskExecutorGateway`

4. `AsyncCheckpointRunnable` finalizes snapshot futures and calls `TaskStateManagerImpl.reportTaskStateSnapshots()` → `checkpointResponder.acknowledgeCheckpoint()` RPC back to the JobMaster
5. `CheckpointCoordinator.receiveAcknowledgeMessage()` calls `PendingCheckpoint.acknowledgeTask()` until `isFullyAcknowledged()`, then `completePendingCheckpoint()` writes metadata, creates `CompletedCheckpoint`, persists to HA store, and broadcasts `notifyCheckpointComplete` to all tasks

### Key Architectural Distinction
- **`SingleCheckpointBarrierHandler`** (EXACTLY_ONCE): Uses a `BarrierHandlerState` state machine; blocks channels during alignment; supports aligned, unaligned (immediate trigger + in-flight buffer capture), and alternating (aligned with timeout fallback to unaligned) modes
- **`CheckpointBarrierTracker`** (AT_LEAST_ONCE): Simply counts barriers without blocking; supports up to 50 concurrent in-flight checkpoints

- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/checkpoint/CheckpointCoordinator.java` — Central JobManager-side coordinator: triggers checkpoints, tracks pending checkpoints, receives acknowledgments, drives completion
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/checkpoint/PendingCheckpoint.java` — Mutable in-flight checkpoint state: tracks which tasks/coordinators/master-hooks have not yet acknowledged; finalizes into CompletedCheckpoint
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/checkpoint/CompletedCheckpoint.java` — Immutable record of a successfully completed checkpoint with all operator state handles and external metadata pointer
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/checkpoint/CheckpointCoordinatorGateway.java` — RPC interface through which TaskManagers send `acknowledgeCheckpoint` and `declineCheckpoint` messages back to the JobMaster
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/checkpoint/CheckpointPlan.java` — Describes which tasks to trigger (sources), which to wait for (all), and which to commit to after completion
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/checkpoint/DefaultCheckpointPlanCalculator.java` — Computes the CheckpointPlan from the ExecutionGraph topology
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/checkpoint/OperatorCoordinatorCheckpoints.java` — Triggers and acknowledges OperatorCoordinator-side checkpoints before task barriers are sent
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/checkpoint/CheckpointRequestDecider.java` — Decides whether a new checkpoint trigger request should execute immediately or be queued, enforcing max-concurrent and min-pause constraints
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/checkpoint/CheckpointFailureManager.java` — Tracks checkpoint failures and decides when to fail the job
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/executiongraph/Execution.java` — Represents a running task attempt; dispatches `triggerCheckpoint` RPC to the TaskManager via `TaskManagerGateway`
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/jobmaster/RpcTaskManagerGateway.java` — Implements `TaskManagerGateway` by delegating to `TaskExecutorGateway` (Akka/Pekko RPC)
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/jobmanager/slots/TaskManagerGateway.java` — Interface for JobManager→TaskManager RPC calls including `triggerCheckpoint`
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/taskexecutor/TaskExecutorGateway.java` — Interface for the TaskExecutor RPC endpoint; receives `triggerCheckpoint` from the JobMaster
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/tasks/StreamTask.java` — Base class for all streaming tasks; implements `CheckpointableTask`; routes `triggerCheckpointOnBarrier` into `SubtaskCheckpointCoordinatorImpl.checkpointState()`
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/tasks/SubtaskCheckpointCoordinatorImpl.java` — Task-side checkpoint orchestrator: broadcasts the barrier downstream, takes synchronous state snapshots, submits async finalization
- `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/tasks/AsyncCheckpointRunnable.java` — Async runnable that finalizes operator snapshot futures and calls `reportCompletedSnapshotStates` to send the ACK
